cfo_workcap.title: Working Capital Cycle
cfo_workcap.askPrompt: My working capital cycle: DIO {dio} days, DSO {dso} days, DPO {dpo} days. Cash Conversion Cycle = {ccc} days. Inventory value {inventory}, receivables {receivables}, payables {payables}. How can I shorten my cash conversion cycle as a {smbLabel}?
cfo_workcap.cccStatusExcellent: Excellent — cash comes in before going out
cfo_workcap.cccStatusLong: Long cycle — cash is tied up for extended periods
cfo_workcap.dioHint: How long inventory sits before being sold
cfo_workcap.dsoHint: How long customers take to pay you
cfo_workcap.dpoHint: How long you take to pay suppliers (longer = better for cash)
cfo_workcap.dioAction1: Run clearance on slow-moving stock (items > 60 days)
cfo_workcap.dioAction2: Reduce reorder quantities for bottom 20% products
cfo_workcap.dioAction3: Move to just-in-time for fast-moving items
cfo_workcap.dsoAction2: Send payment reminders at day 3, 7, and 14
cfo_workcap.dsoAction3: Offer 2% early-payment discount (saves {discount} but accelerates cash)
cfo_workcap.dpoAction1: Negotiate net-30 terms with top 3 suppliers
cfo_workcap.dpoAction2: Consolidate orders for volume-based term extensions
cfo_workcap.dpoAction3: Use scheduled payments instead of immediate settlement
cfo_workcap.negativeCycleTitle: Cash cycle is negative — excellent position
cfo_workcap.negativeCycleImpact: You collect cash before paying suppliers
cfo_workcap.negativeCycleAction1: Maintain supplier relationships to keep these terms
cfo_workcap.negativeCycleAction2: Consider offering early-pay discounts to suppliers for additional savings
cfo_workcap.negativeCycleAction3: Reinvest the float into growth or interest-bearing instruments
cfo_workcap.totalCashTiedLabel: Total cash tied up in cycle
cfo_workcap.cccReductionHint: Reducing CCC by 10 days frees up ~{amount} in working capital
